briefing: OpenAI's Sam Altman apologizes for failing to report a suspect's ChatGPT account to authorities.
Oracle secures $16 billion funding for a massive data center in rural Michigan.
Google's AI agent platform leads the market, though further development is necessary.
Musk drops fraud claims against OpenAI and Altman ahead of their upcoming trial.
SpaceX signs a $60 billion deal with an AI startup founded by MIT dropouts.

articles.title: Nearly half of cybersecurity pros want to quit - here's why
articles.snippet: Research suggests security specialists around the globe are unhappy, but there is hope.
articles.ai_summary: A global survey reveals that nearly half of cybersecurity professionals are dissatisfied with their jobs, highlighting a potential crisis in the industry. However, there are signs of hope for improvement.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals a looming talent crisis in cybersecurity that could impact tech security.

articles.title: I ran the 20-minute Apple Watch calibration test - and my data got more accurate
articles.snippet: Want more accurate, personalized health data during your exercises? Here's how to calibrate your Apple Watch.
articles.ai_summary: Calibrating your Apple Watch can significantly improve the accuracy of health data during workouts. The 20-minute calibration test is recommended for better personalization.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances user experience by optimizing health tracking technology.

articles.title: Our Principles
articles.snippet: Our mission is to ensure that AGI benefits all of humanity. Sam Altman shares five principles that guide our work.
articles.ai_summary: Sam Altman outlines five guiding principles for ensuring that artificial general intelligence (AGI) benefits all of humanity, emphasizing ethical considerations in AI development.
articles.why_it_matters: Establishes a framework for responsible AI development that could influence future policies.

articles.title: AI's not going to kill open source code security
articles.snippet: Cal.com considers AGPL a license to drill, but not everyone feels that way Opinion  Cal.com has closed its commercial codebase, abandoning years of AGPL-3.0 licensing in a move that has alarmed the developer community that helped build it and sent ripples through the broader open source world.…
articles.ai_summary: Cal.com has transitioned from AGPL licensing to a closed commercial codebase, causing concern among developers about the future of open source code security. This shift raises questions about community trust and collaboration.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights tensions between commercial interests and open source values in tech.
